steps.title: Identify the list structure
steps.content: The blank separates several from three methods that must be introduced by punctuation: exchanging information, linking maps, and maintaining a database.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list or elaboration after a complete clause — exactly what follows here (three parallel by-phrases).

A) ways. By — creates a fragment: "By exchanging information, linking maps, and maintaining a database" has no main verb and cannot stand as a sentence. Wrong.
B) ways: by — the clause before the colon is complete; the colon correctly signals the list that follows. Correct.
C) ways; by — a semicolon requires an independent clause on both sides; what follows is a list of phrases, not a clause. Wrong.
D) ways by: — a colon cannot follow a preposition; this is ungrammatical. Wrong.

B is correct.
